lede: A look at July 13 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Alberto Ascari and 1841, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Germany national football team
events.desc: Germany wins the FIFA World Cup, defeating Argentina in the final 1–0 after extra time.

events.title: Typhoon Soulik (2013)
events.desc: Typhoon Soulik kills at least nine people and affects more than 160 million in East China and Taiwan.

events.title: Directorate General for External Security
events.desc: French DGSE personnel abort an operation to rescue Íngrid Betancourt from FARC rebels in Colombia, causing a political scandal when details are leaked to the press.

events.title: Somalia
events.desc: Somalia declares war on Ethiopia, starting the Ogaden War.

events.title: Watergate scandal
events.desc: Watergate scandal: Alexander Butterfield reveals the existence of a secret Oval Office taping system to investigators for the Senate Watergate Committee.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Montenegrins begin the Trinaestojulski ustanak (Thirteenth of July Uprising), a popular revolt against the Axis powers.

events.title: 1930 FIFA World Cup
events.desc: The inaugural FIFA World Cup begins in Uruguay.

events.title: Battle of Trippstadt
events.desc: The Battle of Trippstadt between French forces and those of Prussia and Austria begins.

events.title: English Civil War
events.desc: English Civil War: Battle of Roundway Down: In England, Henry Wilmot, 1st Earl of Rochester, commanding the Royalist forces, heavily defeats the Parliamentarian forces led by Sir William Waller.

events.title: Anglo-Spanish War (1585–1604)
events.desc: Anglo–Spanish War: A convoy of English ships from the Levant Company manage to repel a fleet of eleven Spanish and Maltese galleys off the Mediterranean island of Pantelleria.

births.name: Jurelang Zedkaia
births.line: Marshallese politician, 5th President of the Marshall Islands

births.name: Ma Ying-jeou
births.line: Hong Kong-Taiwanese commander and politician, 12th President of the Republic of China

births.name: Wole Soyinka
births.line: Nigerian author, poet, and playwright, Nobel Prize laureate

deaths.text: Liu Xiaobo, Chinese literary critic, human rights activist
Nadine Gordimer, South African novelist, short story writer, and activist, Nobel Prize laureate
Peter Parler, German architect, designed St. Vitus Cathedral and Charles Bridge

observances.text: Christian feast day: Abel of Tacla Haimonot (Coptic Church)
Christian feast day: Henry II, Holy Roman Emperor
